findings.signature: db.pragma('busy_timeout = 5000'); db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL'); db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON')
findings.notes: Three .pragma() calls at init; Turso lacks .pragma() API — busy_timeout, journal_mode, foreign_keys must be set via connection string or PRAGMA SQL statements.

findings.signature: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS code_files/code_nodes/code_edges/schema_version/code_graph_metadata/parse_diagnostics/parser_skip_list + 11 indexes
findings.notes: Standard DDL — Turso-compatible. CHECK constraints in parser_skip_list need verification but are basic SQLite.

findings.signature: database.exec('ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN ...'); database.exec('CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ...'); database.prepare('INSERT OR IGNORE INTO parser_skip_list ...').run()
findings.notes: Uses database.exec() for multi-statement DDL and PRAGMA table_info() introspection. Turso supports exec() but PRAGMA table_info may need adaptation.

findings.signature: database.prepare('PRAGMA table_info(${tableName})').all()
findings.notes: PRAGMA table_info() introspection; Turso may require alternative column-discovery approach.

findings.signature: const tx = d.transaction(() => { DELETE FROM code_nodes WHERE file_id = ?; DELETE FROM code_edges WHERE source_id IN (...); INSERT OR IGNORE INTO code_nodes ... }); tx()
findings.notes: Synchronous transaction wrapper via better-sqlite3 .transaction() — Turso async driver requires wrapping in async transaction API; DELETE with dynamic placeholders in subquery.

findings.signature: const tx = d.transaction(() => { DELETE FROM code_edges WHERE source_id IN (...); INSERT INTO code_edges ...; DELETE FROM code_edges WHERE source_id NOT IN (SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes) OR target_id NOT IN (...) })
findings.notes: Synchronous transaction with subquery-based dangling-edge prune. Turso async transaction API required; subquery DELETE is standard SQL.

findings.signature: INSERT INTO code_graph_metadata ... ON CONFLICT(key) DO UPDATE SET value = excluded.value, updated_at = excluded.updated_at
findings.notes: UPSERT via ON CONFLICT — standard SQLite, Turso-compatible.

findings.signature: const tx = d.transaction(() => { DELETE FROM code_edges WHERE source_id IN (...) OR target_id IN (...); DELETE FROM code_files WHERE id = ? })
findings.notes: Transaction-wrapped cascade delete; same sync .transaction() coupling as replaceNodes/replaceEdges.

findings.signature: DELETE FROM code_nodes WHERE file_id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM code_files); DELETE FROM code_edges WHERE source_id NOT IN (SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes) OR target_id NOT IN (...)
findings.notes: Subquery-based orphan cleanup — standard SQL, no driver coupling beyond synchronous exec.

findings.signature: WITH filtered_nodes AS (...), aggregated AS (...), ranked AS (SELECT ... ROW_NUMBER() OVER (...)) SELECT ... FROM ranked
findings.notes: CTE with ROW_NUMBER() window function in SELECT. Turso supports CTEs and window functions but this is read-only — lower migration risk.

findings.signature: SELECT ... FROM (... UNION ALL ...) GROUP BY node_file_path
findings.notes: UNION ALL subquery — read-only, standard SQL, Turso-compatible.

findings.signature: DELETE FROM code_edges WHERE source_id NOT IN (SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes) OR target_id NOT IN (SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes)
findings.notes: Subquery-based DELETE — standard SQL, Turso-compatible.

findings.signature: const DB_TRIPLET = ['code-graph.sqlite', 'code-graph.sqlite-wal', 'code-graph.sqlite-shm']
findings.notes: WAL file triplet hardcoded; Turso uses different file layout — WAL/SHM files may not exist or behave differently with remote replicas.

findings.signature: db.pragma('integrity_check') as Array<Record<string, unknown>>
findings.notes: .pragma('integrity_check') returns typed rows — Turso lacks .pragma() API; must use PRAGMA SQL statement and parse differently.

findings.signature: db.pragma('wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)')
findings.notes: WAL checkpoint pragma via .pragma() — Turso has no WAL checkpoint concept for remote replicas; this recovery step must be replaced or skipped.

findings.signature: closeDb(); copyTriplet(...); moveTriplet(...); scan({incremental: false})
findings.notes: Recovery assumes local file-level manipulation of SQLite triplet. Turso remote replicas cannot be quarantined/moved at file level.

findings.signature: renameSync(source, join(targetDir, fileName)) for each of DB_TRIPLET
findings.notes: File-system rename of WAL triplet; Turso remote DB has no local WAL/SHM to move.

findings.signature: handle.prepare('SELECT 1').get() — success means handle is still open
findings.notes: Probes DB handle liveness via prepare().get(); Turso async driver requires await — sync probe pattern breaks.

findings.signature: const tx = graphDb.getDb().transaction(() => { upsertFile(mtime=0); replaceNodes; replaceEdges; upsertFile(real_mtime); clearParseDiagnostic })
findings.notes: Atomic per-file persistence in sync .transaction() — the core write-path unit for scans. Turso async transaction required; 4-statement atomic boundary is critical for crash safety.

findings.signature: graphDb.setCodeGraphMetadata(CANDIDATE_MANIFEST_KEY, JSON.stringify(manifest))
findings.notes: Metadata write via setCodeGraphMetadata — indirect UPSERT, Turso-compatible.

findings.signature: graphDb.removeFile(filePath) in loop
findings.notes: Sequential removeFile calls (each transaction-wrapped) — same sync transaction coupling.

findings.signature: graphDb.setCodeGraphMetadata('last_git_head', head) via setMetadata
findings.notes: Simple metadata write — Turso-compatible.

findings.signature: snapshotKnownGoodTriplet({dbDir, auditDir, now}) — copies WAL triplet
findings.notes: Pre-apply snapshot copies DB triplet (including WAL/SHM). Turso remote replicas cannot be snapshot at file level.

findings.signature: switch(operation) { case 'recover-sqlite-corruption': recoverSqliteCorruption(...); case 'rollback-bad-apply': rollbackBadApply(...) }
findings.notes: Apply orchestrator dispatches to file-level recovery procedures; both assume local SQLite triplet manipulation incompatible with Turso.

findings.signature: appendFileSync(path, JSON.stringify({...}) + '\n', { mode: 0o600 })
findings.notes: Audit log writes to filesystem, not SQLite — no migration impact.

findings.signature: preflight battery -> snapshotKnownGoodTriplet -> dispatchOperation -> postflight battery -> rollbackBadApply on failure
findings.notes: Full apply lifecycle depends on file-level snapshot/rollback. Turso migration needs alternative rollback strategy (e.g., logical undo or point-in-time recovery).
